---
title: "Gallery"
description: |
  I started painting more regularly at the onset of the pandemic and am still very much an amateur. Most of these are acrylic on stretched canvas, unless otherwise noted. Some are available in my Redbubble shop!
output:
  distill::distill_article:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

#### [Redbubble shop](https://www.redbubble.com/people/alexandglia/shop?asc=u)

My partner and I had covid-19 a couple weeks before it was reported that cats could get sick, too.  
![Cauchy in a mask](IMG_6941.jpg)
  
Cauchy screams (or yawns)    
![He screams!](IMG_6762.jpg)    
  

## Zebrafish art

The zebrafish spinal cord, viewed laterally but imagined underwater, complete with octodendrocytes and a seafloor plant Mauthner.  
![Octodendrocytes!](IMG_6972.jpg)  

![Zebrafish embryos in chorions!](IMG_6719.jpg)  

![Adult zebrafish](IMG_6761.jpg)  
    
    
Can you spot the zebrafish? A Denver zebrafish starry night.
![A Denver zebrafish starry night](IMG_7192.jpg)  
  
  
![Geometric zebrafish bbs](IMG_6998(1).jpg)  

## Microglia art  

Tectal microglia, here isolated (n=9 cells from each of 9 larvae) and below hugging neuronal somas.
![Tectal microglia](IMG_6844.jpg)
  
![Tectal microglia and neurons!](IMG_6987.jpg)
  
## Oligodendrocyte art  
    
Bought a clock kit to timelapse-image oligodendrocytes at home! A sheath per hour is about 2.4 fold **slower** than this occurs in fish (5 hours for all sheaths). It's always oligo'clock!
![It's always oligo'clock!](IMG_7040.jpg)  

  
Making more functional things. These are glia coasters (L to R): NG2, radial glia, microglia, oligodendrocyte.
![Glia coasters: NG2, radial glia, microglia, oligodendrocyte](IMG_7105.jpg)  

  
When I started writing my dissertation (esp acknowledgments!) it was evident that I was going to need tissues.
![Thesis tissue box for crying](IMG_7150.jpg)  

  
Oligodendrocytes without axons (first attempt)  
![Oligodendrocytes-1](IMG_6900.jpg)  

  
  
Oligodendrocytes and axons, playing with depth (much improved)    
![Oligodendrocytes-2](IMG_7454.jpg)  

    
## Not science art

![Flowers](IMG_6769.jpg)  
 
![Triangles](IMG_6823.jpg)  

A little too much time to reminisce during WFH. Lucy and Pearl, my pet birds (cockatiels) that I had as a kid.
![Lucy and Pearl](IMG_6970.jpg)  

I cut a couple square canvases and painted them as Zoom frames for a very 2020 Halloween. 
![Halloween 2020](zoomframe.jpg)
